turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Mel! Good to see you! How have you been?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline! Good to see you! I'm swamped with the kids & work. What's up with you? Anything new?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I went to a LGBTQ support group yesterday and it was so powerful.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, that's cool, Caroline! What happened that was so awesome? Did you hear any inspiring stories?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: The transgender stories were so inspiring! I was so happy and thankful for all the support. [image: a photo of a dog walking past a wall with a painting of a woman]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a dog walking past a wall with a painting of a woman

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, love that painting! So cool you found such a helpful group. What's it done for you?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: The support group has made me feel accepted and given me courage to embrace myself.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's really cool. You've got guts. What now?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Gonna continue my edu and check out career options, which is pretty exciting!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! What kinda jobs are you thinkin' of? Anything that stands out?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I'm keen on counseling or working in mental health - I'd love to support those with similar issues.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: You'd be a great counselor! Your empathy and understanding will really help the people you work with. By the way, take a look at this. [image: a photo of a painting of a sunset over a lake]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a sunset over a lake

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! That's really sweet. Is this your own painting?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, I painted that lake sunrise last year! It's special to me.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Melanie! The colors really blend nicely. Painting looks like a great outlet for expressing yourself.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Painting's a fun way to express my feelings and get creative. It's a great way to relax after a long day.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Totally agree, Mel. Relaxing and expressing ourselves is key. Well, I'm off to go do some research.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yep, Caroline. Taking care of ourselves is vital. I'm off to go swimming with the kids. Talk to you soon!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline, since we last chatted, I've had a lot of things happening to me. I ran a charity race for mental health last Saturday – it was really rewarding. Really made me think about taking care of our minds.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That charity race sounds great, Mel! Making a difference & raising awareness for mental health is super rewarding - I'm really proud of you for taking part!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! The event was really thought-provoking. I'm starting to realize that self-care is really important. It's a journey for me, but when I look after myself, I'm able to better look after my family.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I totally agree, Melanie. Taking care of ourselves is so important - even if it's not always easy. Great that you're prioritizing self-care.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, it's tough. So I'm carving out some me-time each day - running, reading, or playing my violin - which refreshes me and helps me stay present for my fam!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That's great, Mel! Taking time for yourself is so important. You're doing an awesome job looking after yourself and your family!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline. It's still a work in progress, but I'm doing my best. My kids are so excited about summer break! We're thinking about going camping next month. Any fun plans for the summer?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Researching adoption agencies — it's been a dream to have a family and give a loving home to kids who need it.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! That's awesome! Taking in kids in need - you're so kind. Your future family is gonna be so lucky to have you!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! My goal is to give kids a loving home. I'm truly grateful for all the support I've got from friends and mentors. Now the hard work starts to turn my dream into a reality. And here's one of the adoption agencies I'm looking into. It's a lot to take in, but I'm feeling hopeful and optimistic. [image: a photography of a sign for a new arrival and an information and domestic building]
turns.image_caption: a photography of a sign for a new arrival and an information and domestic building

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, that agency looks great! What made you pick it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I chose them 'cause they help LGBTQ+ folks with adoption. Their inclusivity and support really spoke to me.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's great, Caroline! Loving the inclusivity and support. Anything you're excited for in the adoption process?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I'm thrilled to make a family for kids who need one. It'll be tough as a single parent, but I'm up for the challenge!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: You're doing something amazing! Creating a family for those kids is so lovely. You'll be an awesome mom! Good luck!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! Your kind words really mean a lot. I'll do my best to make sure these kids have a safe and loving home.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: No doubts, Caroline. You have such a caring heart - they'll get all the love and stability they need! Excited for this new chapter!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Melanie! How's it going? I wanted to tell you about my school event last week. It was awesome! I talked about my transgender journey and encouraged students to get involved in the LGBTQ community. It was great to see their reactions. It made me reflect on how far I've come since I started transitioning three years ago.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline! Great to hear from you. Sounds like your event was amazing! I'm so proud of you for spreading awareness and getting others involved in the LGBTQ community. You've come a long way since your transition - keep on inspiring people with your strength and courage!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! Your backing really means a lot. I felt super powerful giving my talk. I shared my own journey, the struggles I had and how much I've developed since coming out. It was wonderful to see how the audience related to what I said and how it inspired them to be better allies. Conversations about gender identity and inclusion are so necessary and I'm thankful for being able to give a voice to the trans community.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, you're doing an awesome job of inspiring others with your journey. It's great to be part of it and see how you're positively affecting so many. Talking about inclusivity and acceptance is crucial, and you're so brave to speak up for the trans community. Keep up the great work!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks Mel! Your kind words mean a lot. Sharing our experiences isn't always easy, but I feel it's important to help promote understanding and acceptance. I've been blessed with loads of love and support throughout this journey, and I want to pass it on to others. By sharing our stories, we can build a strong, supportive community of hope.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, Caroline! It takes courage to talk about our own stories. But it's in these vulnerable moments that we bond and understand each other. We all have our different paths, but if we share them, we show people that they're not alone. Our stories can be so inspiring and encouraging to others who are facing the same challenges. Thank you for using your voice to create love, acceptance, and hope. You're doing amazing!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Your words mean a lot to me. I'm grateful for the chance to share my story and give others hope. We all have unique paths, and by working together we can build a more inclusive and understanding world. I'm going to keep using my voice to make a change and lift others up. And you're part of that!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline, for letting me join your journey. I'm so proud to be part of the difference you're making. Let's keep motivating and helping each other out as we journey through life. We can make a real impact together!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yeah Mel, let's spread love and understanding! Thanks for the support and encouragement. We can tackle life's challenges together! We got this!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yes, Caroline! We can do it. Your courage is inspiring. I want to be couragous for my family- they motivate me and give me love. What motivates you?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! My friends, family and mentors are my rocks – they motivate me and give me the strength to push on. Here's a pic from when we met up last week! [image: a photo of a family posing for a picture in a yard]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a family posing for a picture in a yard

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, that photo is great! How long have you had such a great support system?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yeah, I'm really lucky to have them. They've been there through everything, I've known these friends for 4 years, since I moved from my home country. Their love and help have been so important especially after that tough breakup. I'm super thankful. Who supports you, Mel?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I'm lucky to have my husband and kids; they keep me motivated. [image: a photo of a man and a little girl standing in front of a waterfall]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a man and a little girl standing in front of a waterfall

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, what an amazing family pic! How long have you been married?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: 5 years already! Time flies- feels like just yesterday I put this dress on! Thanks, Caroline! [image: a photo of a bride in a wedding dress holding a bouquet]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a bride in a wedding dress holding a bouquet

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Congrats, Melanie! You both looked so great on your wedding day! Wishing you many happy years together!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Appreciate your kind words. Looking forward to more happy years. Our family and moments make it all worth it. [image: a photo of a man and woman sitting on a blanket eating food]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a man and woman sitting on a blanket eating food

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Looks like you had a great day! How was it? You all look so happy!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: It so fun! We played games, ate good food, and just hung out together. Family moments make life awesome.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Sounds great, Mel! Glad you had a great time. Cherish the moments - they're the best!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Absolutely, Caroline! I cherish time with family. It's when I really feel alive and happy.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I 100% agree, Mel. Hanging with loved ones is amazing and brings so much happiness. Those moments really make me thankful. Family is everything.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Melanie! Long time no talk! A lot's been going on in my life! Take a look at this. [image: a photo of a person holding a necklace with a cross and a heart]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a person holding a necklace with a cross and a heart

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey, Caroline! Nice to hear from you! Love the necklace, any special meaning to it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! This necklace is super special to me - a gift from my grandma in my home country, Sweden. She gave it to me when I was young, and it stands for love, faith and strength. It's like a reminder of my roots and all the love and support I get from my family.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's gorgeous, Caroline! It's awesome what items can mean so much to us, right? Got any other objects that you treasure, like that necklace?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a stack of bowls with different designs on them

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yep, Melanie! I've got some other stuff with sentimental value, like my hand-painted bowl. A friend made it for my 18th birthday ten years ago. The pattern and colors are awesome-- it reminds me of art and self-expression.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That sounds great, Caroline! It's awesome having stuff around that make us think of good connections and times. Actually, I just took my fam camping in the mountains last week - it was a really nice time together!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Sounds great, Mel. Glad you made some new family mems. How was it? Anything fun?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: It was an awesome time, Caroline! We explored nature, roasted marshmallows around the campfire and even went on a hike. The view from the top was amazing! The 2 younger kids love nature. It was so special having these moments together as a family - I'll never forget it!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That's awesome, Melanie! Family moments like that are so special. Glad y'all had such a great time.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Family time matters to me. What's up with you lately?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Lately, I've been looking into counseling and mental health as a career. I want to help people who have gone through the same things as me.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a book shelf with many books on it

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Sounds great! What kind of counseling and mental health services do you want to persue?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I'm still figuring out the details, but I'm thinking of working with trans people, helping them accept themselves and supporting their mental health. Last Friday, I went to an LGBTQ+ counseling workshop and it was really enlightening. They talked about different therapeutic methods and how to best work with trans people. Seeing how passionate these pros were about making a safe space for people like me was amazing.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Woah, Caroline, it sounds like you're doing some impressive work. It's inspiring to see your dedication to helping others. What motivated you to pursue counseling?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie. It really mattered. My own journey and the support I got made a huge difference. Now I want to help people go through it too. I saw how counseling and support groups improved my life, so I started caring more about mental health and understanding myself. Now I'm passionate about creating a safe, inviting place for people to grow.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! You've gained so much from your own experience. Your passion and hard work to help others is awesome. Keep it up, you're making a big impact!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! Your kind words mean a lot.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Congrats Caroline! Good on you for going after what you really care about.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a book shelf filled with books in a room

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Since we last spoke, some big things have happened. Last week I went to an LGBTQ+ pride parade. Everyone was so happy and it made me feel like I belonged. It showed me how much our community has grown, it was amazing!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, sounds like the parade was an awesome experience! It's great to see the love and support for the LGBTQ+ community. Congrats! Has this experience influenced your goals at all?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! It really motivated me for sure. Talking to the community made me want to use my story to help others too - I'm still thinking that counseling and mental health is the way to go. I'm super excited to give back.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! That's great! I just signed up for a pottery class yesterday. It's like therapy for me, letting me express myself and get creative. Have you found any activities that make you feel the same way? [image: a photo of a person holding a frisbee in their hand]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a person holding a frisbee in their hand

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Melanie! I'm getting creative too, just learning the piano. What made you try pottery?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I'm a big fan of pottery - the creativity and skill is awesome. Plus, making it is so calming. Look at this! [image: a photo of a bowl with a black and white flower design]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a bowl with a black and white flower design

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That bowl is gorgeous! The black and white design looks so fancy. Did you make it?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Yeah, I made this bowl in my class. It took some work, but I'm pretty proud of it.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Nice job! You really put in the work and it definitely shows. Your creativity looks great!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Your kind words mean a lot. Pottery is a huge part of my life, not just a hobby - it helps me express my emotions. Clay is incredible, it brings me so much joy!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel, I'm so stoked for you that art is helping you express yourself and bring you joy! Keep it up!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! I'm excited to see where pottery takes me. Anything coming up you're looking forward to?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks Mel! I'm going to a transgender conference this month. I'm so excited to meet other people in the community and learn more about advocacy. It's gonna be great!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Sounds awesome, Caroline! Have a great time and learn a lot. Have fun!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Cool, thanks Mel! Can't wait. I'll keep ya posted. Bye!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Bye, Caroline! Can't wait to hear about it. Have fun and stay safe!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Mel! Long time no talk. Lots has been going on since then!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline! Missed you. Anything new? Spill the beans!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Since our last chat, I've been looking into counseling or mental health work more. I'm passionate about helping people and making a positive impact. It's tough, but really rewarding too. Anything new happening with you?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's awesome, Caroline! Congrats on following your dreams. Yesterday I took the kids to the museum - it was so cool spending time with them and seeing their eyes light up! [image: a photography of two children playing in a water play area]
turns.image_caption: a photography of two children playing in a water play area

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Melanie, that's a great pic! That must have been awesome. What were they so stoked about?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: They were stoked for the dinosaur exhibit! They love learning about animals and the bones were so cool. It reminds me why I love being a mom.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Being a mom is awesome. I'm creating a library for when I have kids. I'm really looking forward to reading to them and opening up their minds. [image: a photo of a bookcase filled with books and toys]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a bookcase filled with books and toys

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Sounds great! What kind of books you got in your library?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I've got lots of kids' books- classics, stories from different cultures, educational books, all of that. What's a favorite book you remember from your childhood?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I loved reading "Charlotte's Web" as a kid. It was so cool seeing how friendship and compassion can make a difference. [image: a photo of a book cover with a picture of a girl and a cat]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a book cover with a picture of a girl and a cat

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, that's great! It sure shows how important friendship and compassion are. It's made me appreciate how lucky I am to have my friends and family helping with my transition. They make all the difference. We even had a picnic last week! [image: a photo of a group of women sitting on a blanket in a park]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a group of women sitting on a blanket in a park

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's a gorgeous photo, Caroline! Wow, the love around you is awesome. How have your friends and fam been helping you out with your transition?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! This support network has been amazing. They've been there for me every step of the way giving me love, guidance, and acceptance. I couldn't have done it without them.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! It's great you have people to support you, that's really awesome!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I'm so lucky to have such a great support system around me. Their love and encouragement has really helped me accept and grow into my true self. They've been instrumental in my transition.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Glad you have support, Caroline! Unconditional love is so important. Here's a pic of my family camping at the beach. We love it, it brings us closer! [image: a photo of a family sitting around a campfire on the beach]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a family sitting around a campfire on the beach

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Mel, great to chat with you again! So much has happened since we last spoke - I went to an LGBTQ conference two days ago and it was really special. I got the chance to meet and connect with people who've gone through similar journeys. It was such a welcoming environment and I felt totally accepted. I'm really thankful for this amazing community - it's shown me how important it is to fight for trans rights and spread awareness.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that sounds awesome! So glad you felt accepted and supported. Events like these are great for reminding us of how strong community can be!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yeah, it's true! Having people who back you makes such a huge difference. It's great to see how far LGBTQ rights have come, but there's still plenty of progress to be made. I wanna help make a difference.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline. We've come so far, but there's more to do. Your drive to help is awesome! What's your plan to pitch in?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mell! I'm still looking into counseling and mental health jobs. It's important to me that people have someone to talk to, and I want to help make that happen.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! You're so inspiring for wanting to help others with their mental health. What's pushing you to keep going forward with it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I struggled with mental health, and support I got was really helpful. It made me realize how important it is for others to have a support system. So, I started looking into counseling and mental health career options, so I could help other people on their own journeys like I was helped.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Caroline, so glad you got the support! Your experience really brought you to where you need to be. You're gonna make a huge difference! This book I read last year reminds me to always pursue my dreams, just like you are doing!🌟 [image: a photography of a book cover with a gold coin on it]
turns.image_caption: a photography of a book cover with a gold coin on it

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks so much, Mel! Seeing this pic just made me appreciate my love of reading even more. Books guide me, motivate me and help me discover who I am. They're a huge part of my journey, and this one's reminding me to keep going and never give up!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! Books have such an awesome power! Which one has been your favorite guide?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I loved "Becoming Nicole" by Amy Ellis Nutt. It's a real inspiring true story about a trans girl and her family. It made me feel connected and gave me a lot of hope for my own path. Highly recommend it for sure! [image: a photo of a dog sitting in a boat on the water]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a dog sitting in a boat on the water

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That sounds awesome! What did you take away from it to use in your life?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: It taught me self-acceptance and how to find support. It also showed me that tough times don't last - hope and love exist. Pets bring so much joy too, though.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Caroline, those lessons are great - self-acceptance and finding support are key. Plus pets are awesome for joy and comfort, can't agree more! [image: a photography of two little girls sitting on the steps with a dog]
turns.image_caption: a photography of two little girls sitting on the steps with a dog

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That's so nice! What pet do you have?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: We've got a pup and a kitty. That's the dog, and here's our cat! They brighten up our day and always make us smile. [image: a photo of a cat laying on the floor with its head on the floor]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a cat laying on the floor with its head on the floor

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Ah, they're adorable! What are their names? Pets sure do bring so much joy to us!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Luna and Oliver! They are so sweet and playful - they really liven up the house! Just got some new shoes, too! [image: a photo of a person wearing pink sneakers on a white rug]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a person wearing pink sneakers on a white rug

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Love that purple color! For walking or running?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! These are for running. Been running longer since our last chat - a great way to destress and clear my mind.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a pair of pink sneakers in a box

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow! What got you into running?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I've been running farther to de-stress, which has been great for my headspace.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Cool, Melanie! Running can really boost your mood. Keep it up!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! This has been great for my mental health. I'm gonna keep it up.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Awesome, Melanie! Mental health's a priority, so make sure you take care of yourself.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Caroline, thanks! Mental health is important to me, and it's made such an improvement!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Glad it helped ya, Melanie!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Mel, what's up? Been a busy week since we talked.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline, it's been super busy here. So much since we talked! Last Fri I finally took my kids to a pottery workshop. We all made our own pots, it was fun and therapeutic! [image: a photography of a group of children making clay sculptures in a classroom]
turns.image_caption: a photography of a group of children making clay sculptures in a classroom

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel! Sounds like you and the kids had a blast. How'd they like it?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: The kids loved it! They were so excited to get their hands dirty and make something with clay. It was special to watch their creativity and imagination come to life, they made this! [image: a photo of a cup with a dog face on it]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a cup with a dog face on it

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Aww, that's so sweet! That cup is so cute. It's awesome to see how kids show their personalities through art. What other creative projects do you do with them, besides pottery?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: We love painting together lately, especially nature-inspired ones. Here's our latest work from last weekend. [image: a photo of a painting of a sunset with a palm tree]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a sunset with a palm tree

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow Mel, that painting's amazing! The colors are so bold and it really highlights the beauty of nature. Y'all work on it together?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! We both helped with the painting - it was great bonding over it and chatting about nature. We found these lovely flowers. Appreciating the small things in life, too. [image: a photo of a field of purple flowers with green leaves]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a field of purple flowers with green leaves

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That photo is stunning! So glad you bonded over our love of nature. Last Friday I went to a council meeting for adoption. It was inspiring and emotional - so many people wanted to create loving homes for children in need. It made me even more determined to adopt.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, way to go! Your future fam will get a kick out of having you. What do you think of these? [image: a photo of a blue vase with a bouquet of sunflowers and roses]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a blue vase with a bouquet of sunflowers and roses

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks Melanie - love the blue vase in the pic! Blue's my fave, it makes me feel relaxed. Sunflowers mean warmth and happiness, right? While roses stand for love and beauty? That's neat. What do flowers mean to you?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Flowers bring joy. They represent growth, beauty and reminding us to appreciate the small moments. They were an important part of my wedding decor and always remind me of that day. [image: a photo of a row of white chairs with flowers on them]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a row of white chairs with flowers on them

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: It must have been special at your wedding. I wish I had known you back then!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: It was amazing, Caroline. The day was full of love and joy. Everyone we love was there to celebrate us - it was really special. [image: a photo of a wedding ceremony in a greenhouse with people taking pictures]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a wedding ceremony in a greenhouse with people taking pictures

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, what a great day! Glad everyone could make it. What was your favorite part?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Marrying my partner and promising to be together forever was the best part. [image: a photo of a man and woman standing on a beach]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a man and woman standing on a beach

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, nice pic! You both looked amazing. One special memory for me was this pride parade I went to a few weeks ago. [image: a photo of a parade with people walking down the street]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a parade with people walking down the street

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, looks awesome! Did you join in?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yes, I did. It was amazing! I felt so accepted and happy, just being around people who accepted and celebrated me. It's definitely a top memory. [image: a photo of a group of people holding up signs and smiling]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a group of people holding up signs and smiling

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, what an experience! How did it make you feel?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I felt so proud and grateful - the vibes were amazing and it was comforting to know I'm not alone and have a great community around me.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a rainbow flag on a pole on a carpet

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! That's huge! How did it feel to be around so much love and acceptance?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: It was awesome, Melanie! Being around people who embrace and back me up is beyond words. It really inspired me.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a group of people sitting on the ground with a dog

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, that sounds awesome! Your friends and community really have your back. What's been the best part of it?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a girl sitting in a teepee with stuffed animals

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Realizing I can be me without fear and having the courage to transition was the best part. It's so freeing to express myself authentically and have people back me up.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a teepee with a teddy bear and pillows

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's awesome, Caro! You've found the courage to be yourself - that's important for our mental health and finding peace.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a buddha statue and a candle on a table

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! Been a long road, but I'm proud of how far I've come. How're you doing finding peace?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I'm getting there, Caroline. Creativity and family keep me at peace.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a man holding a frisbee in front of a frisbee golf basket

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That's awesome, Melanie! How have your family been supportive during your move?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: My fam's been awesome - they helped out and showed lots of love and support.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel, family love and support is the best!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, Caroline, my family's been great - their love and support really helped me through tough times. It's awesome! We even went on another camping trip in the forest. [image: a photo of a man and two children sitting around a campfire]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a man and two children sitting around a campfire

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Awesome, Mel! Family support's huge. What else do you guys like doing together?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a family walking through a forest with a toddler

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: We enjoy hiking in the mountains and exploring forests. It's a cool way to connect with nature and each other.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel, that sounds awesome! Exploring nature and family time is so special.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, Caroline, they're some of my fave memories. It brings us together and brings us happiness. Glad you're here to share in it.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! Really glad to have you as a friend to share my journey. You're awesome!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Appreciate your friendship. It's great to have a supporter!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: No worries, Mel! Your friendship means so much to me. Enjoy your day!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline, hope all's good! I had a quiet weekend after we went camping with my fam two weekends ago. It was great to unplug and hang with the kids. What've you been up to? Anything fun over the weekend?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Melanie! That sounds great! Last weekend I joined a mentorship program for LGBTQ youth - it's really rewarding to help the community.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! It's great that you're helping out. How's it going? Got any cool experiences you can share?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: The mentoring is going great! I've met some amazing young folks and supported them along the way. It's inspiring to see how resilient and strong they are.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that sounds super rewarding! Young people's resilience is amazing. Care to share some stories?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I mentor a transgender teen just like me. We've been working on building up confidence and finding positive strategies, and it's really been paying off! We had a great time at the LGBT pride event last month.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Caroline, awesome news that you two are getting along! What was it like for you both? Care to fill me in?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: The pride event was awesome! It was so encouraging to be surrounded by so much love and acceptance. [image: a photo of a woman holding a rainbow umbrella in the air]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a woman holding a rainbow umbrella in the air

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow! What's the best part you remember from it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Seeing my mentee's face light up when they saw the support was the best! Such a special moment.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! They must have felt so appreciated. It's awesome to see the difference we can make in each other's lives. Any other exciting LGBTQ advocacy stuff coming up?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yay! Next month I'm having an LGBTQ art show with my paintings - can't wait!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that sounds awesome! Can't wait to see your art - got any previews?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting with a blue and yellow design

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Check out my painting for the art show! Hope you like it. [image: a photography of a painting of a tree with a bright sun in the background]
turns.image_caption: a photography of a painting of a tree with a bright sun in the background

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that painting is awesome! Those colors are so vivid and the whole thing looks really unified. What inspired you?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! I painted this after I visited a LGBTQ center. I wanted to capture everyone's unity and strength.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! It really conveys unity and strength - such a gorgeous piece! My kids and I just finished another painting like our last one.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Melanie! Just wanted to say hi!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline! Good to talk to you again. What's up? Anything new since last time?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Mel! A lot's happened since we last chatted - I just joined a new LGBTQ activist group last Tues. I'm meeting so many cool people who are as passionate as I am about rights and community support. I'm giving my voice and making a real difference, plus it's fulfilling in so many ways. It's just great, you know?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's awesome, Caroline! Glad to hear you found a great group where you can have an impact. Bet it feels great to be able to speak your truth and stand up for what's right. Want to tell me a bit more about it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! It's awesome to have our own platform to be ourselves and support others' rights. Our group, 'Connected LGBTQ Activists', is made of all kinds of people investing in positive changes. We have regular meetings, plan events and campaigns, to get together and support each other.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, your group sounds awesome! Supporting each other and making good things happen - that's so inspiring! Have you been part of any events or campaigns lately?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Last weekend our city held a pride parade! So many people marched through the streets waving flags, holding signs and celebrating love and diversity. I missed it but it was a powerful reminder that we are not alone in this fight for equality and inclusivity. Change is possible!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, fantastic, Caroline! Bet the atmosphere was incredible. Oh yeah, we went to the beach recently. It was awesome! The kids had such a blast. [image: a photo of three children playing on the beach with a kite]
turns.image_caption: a photo of three children playing on the beach with a kite

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Sounds fun! What was the best part? Do you do it often with the kids?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Seeing my kids' faces so happy at the beach was the best! We don't go often, usually only once or twice a year. But those times are always special to spend time together and chill.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a sand castle on the beach with a blue sky

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Sounds special, those beach trips! Do you have any other summer traditions you all do together? Create those memories!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: We always look forward to our family camping trip. We roast marshmallows, tell stories around the campfire and just enjoy each other's company. It's the highlight of our summer! [image: a photo of a fire pit with a lot of fire and sparks]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a fire pit with a lot of fire and sparks

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel, that's awesome! What's your best camping memory?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I'll always remember our camping trip last year when we saw the Perseid meteor shower. It was so amazing lying there and watching the sky light up with streaks of light. We all made wishes and felt so at one with the universe. That's a memory I'll never forget. [image: a photo of a plane flying in the sky with a star filled sky]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a plane flying in the sky with a star filled sky

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Cool! What did it look like?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: The sky was so clear and filled with stars, and the meteor shower was amazing - it felt like we were part of something huge and awe-inspiring. [image: a photo of a plane flying in the sky with a trail of smoke coming out of it]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a plane flying in the sky with a trail of smoke coming out of it

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel. That must've been breathtaking!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: It was one of those moments where I felt tiny and in awe of the universe. Reminds me how awesome life is - so many little moments like that.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a beach with footprints in the sand and a blue sky

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That's great, Mel! What other good memories do you have that make you feel thankful for life?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I'll never forget the day my youngest took her first steps. Seeing her wobble as she took those initial steps really put into perspective how fleeting life is and how lucky I am to be able to share these moments.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Aw, that's sweet, Mel! Those milestones are great reminders of how special our bonds are.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a baby in a white crib with a blue blanket

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, they sure are. It's special moments like these that make me appreciate life and how lucky I am to be with my family and have our love. [image: a photography of a family standing on the beach at sunset]
turns.image_caption: a photography of a family standing on the beach at sunset

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Melanie, what a beautiful moment! Lucky you to have such an awesome family!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! I'm really lucky to have my family; they bring so much joy and love.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline! Last night was amazing! We celebrated my daughter's birthday with a concert surrounded by music, joy and the warm summer breeze. Seeing my kids' smiles was so awesome, and I'm so thankful for our special moments together.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, sounds wonderful! Your love for your kids is so awesome. What concert was it? The advocacy event was a cool experience - so much love and support, amazing!
turns.image_caption: a photo of a poster for a concert with a picture of a man

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! It was Matt Patterson, he is so talented! His voice and songs were amazing. What's up with you? Anything interesting going on?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel, glad you had a blast at the concert. A lot's happened since we talked. I went to a pride parade last Friday and it was awesome - so much energy and love everywhere. Really made me proud and reminded me how important it is to keep standing up for equality.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, that's awesome! How did it feel being part of that community?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a band performing on stage with a sign that says all are welcome

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: It was so inspiring, Mel! Check out the crowd. People of all kinds celebrating love and acceptance - it really pushed me to keep fighting for LGBTQ rights. [image: a photo of a group of people walking down a street with balloons]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a group of people walking down a street with balloons

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! That sounds awesome. This pic's from last night - looks like everyone was having a blast! Reminds me it's important to cultivate a loving and accepting environment for our kids. How do you stay inclusive in your work as an artist? [image: a photo of a group of people sitting on chairs watching a band]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a group of people sitting on chairs watching a band

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That pic is cool! Representing inclusivity and diversity in my art is important to me. I also use it to speak up for the LGBTQ+ community and push for acceptance. Here's a recent painting! [image: a photo of a painting with a painting brush and paint on it]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting with a painting brush and paint on it

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, that rocks! What's the main idea of your art?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: My art is about expressing my trans experience. It's my way of showing my story and helping people understand the trans community.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a woman with a cow in her lap

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Your art's amazing, Caroline. I love how you use it to tell your stories and teach people about trans folks. I'd love to see another painting of yours!
turns.image_caption: a photo of a person holding a purple bowl in their hand

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie. Here's one- 'Embracing Identity' is all about finding comfort and love in being yourself. The woman in the painting stands for the journey of acceptance. My aim was to show warmth, love and self-acceptance. [image: a photo of a painting of a woman with a red shirt]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a woman with a red shirt

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that's gorgeous! I love the self-acceptance and love theme. How does art help you with your self-discovery and acceptance journey?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a woman is making a vase on a wheel

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Art's allowed me to explore my transition and my changing body. It's been a great way to work through stuff I'm going through. I love that it teaches me to accept the beauty of imperfections.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that's so cool! Art can be so healing and a way to really connect with who you are. It's awesome that beauty can be found in the imperfections. We're all individual and wonderfully imperfect. Thanks for sharing it with me!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie. It means a lot to share this with you.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Great chatting with you! Feel free to reach out any time.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Mel! How're ya doin'? Recently, I had a not-so-great experience on a hike. I ran into a group of religious conservatives who said something that really upset me. It made me think how much work we still have to do for LGBTQ rights. It's been so helpful to have people around me who accept and support me, so I know I'll be ok!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline, sorry about the hike. It sucks when people are so closed-minded. Strong support really helps. FYI, I finished another pottery project - want to see a pic?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Sure thing, Melanie! Can't wait to see your pottery project.  I'm happy you found something that makes you happy. Show me when you can!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Here it is. Pretty proud of it! It was a great experience. Thoughts? [image: a photo of a bowl with a colorful design on it]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a bowl with a colorful design on it

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That bowl is awesome, Mel! What gave you the idea for all the colors and patterns?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! I'm obsessed with those, so I made something to catch the eye and make people smile. Plus, painting helps me express my feelings and be creative. Each stroke carries a part of me.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That's amazing! You put so much effort and passion into it. Your creativity really shines. Seeing how art can be a source of self-expression and growth is truly inspiring. You're killing it!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Your words really mean a lot. I've always felt a strong connection to art, and it's been a huge learning experience. It's both a sanctuary and a source of comfort. I'm so glad to have something that brings me so much happiness and fulfillment.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Glad you found something that makes you so happy! Surrounding ourselves with things that bring us joy is important. Life's too short to do anything else!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Agreed, Caroline. Life's tough but it's worth it when we have things that make us happy.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Definitely, Mel! Finding those happy moments and clinging to them is key. It's what keeps us going, even when life's hard. I'm lucky to have people like you to remind me.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, same here Caroline. You make life's struggles more bearable.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! It means a lot having you in my corner. Appreciate our friendship!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I appreciate our friendship too, Caroline. You've always been there for me.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I'm always here for you, Mel! We had a blast last year at the Pride fest. Those supportive friends definitely make everything worth it! [image: a photo of a group of people walking down a street with balloons]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a group of people walking down a street with balloons

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That was a blast! So much fun with the whole gang! Wanna do a family outing this summer?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Right, it was so much fun! We could do a family outting, or wanna plan something special for this summer, just us two? It'd be a great chance to catch up and explore nature! What do you think?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Sounds great, Caroline! Let's plan something special!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Sounds great, Mel! We'll make some awesome memories!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, Caroline! I'll start thinking about what we can do.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yeah, Mel! Life's all about creating memories. Can't wait for the trip!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hi Melanie! Hope you're doing good. Guess what I did this week? I took the first step towards becoming a mom - I applied to adoption agencies! It's a big decision, but I think I'm ready to give all my love to a child. I got lots of help from this adoption advice/assistance group I attended. It was great! [image: a photo of a sign with a picture of a guinea pig]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a sign with a picture of a guinea pig

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Caroline, congrats! So proud of you for taking this step. How does it feel? Also, do you have any pets?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! Exciting but kinda nerve-wracking. Parenting's such a big responsibility. And yup, I do- Oscar, my guinea pig. He's been great. How are your pets?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, it's normal to be both excited and nervous with a big decision. And thanks for asking, they're good- we got another cat named Bailey too. Here's a pic of Oliver. Can you show me one of Oscar? [image: a photo of a black dog laying in the grass with a frisbee]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a black dog laying in the grass with a frisbee

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: He's so cute! What’s the funniest thing Oliver's done? And sure, check out this pic of him eating parsley! Veggies are his fave! [image: a photography of a guinea in a cage with hay and hay]
turns.image_caption: a photography of a guinea in a cage with hay and hay

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Oliver's hilarious! He hid his bone in my slipper once! Cute, right? Almost as silly as when I got to feed a horse a carrot. [image: a photo of a person holding a carrot in front of a horse]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a person holding a carrot in front of a horse

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That's so funny! I used to go horseback riding with my dad when I was a kid, we'd go through the fields, feeling the wind. It was so special. I've always had a love for horses!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, that sounds great - I agree, they're awesome. Here's a photo of my horse painting I did recently. [image: a photo of a horse painted on a wooden wall]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a horse painted on a wooden wall

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Melanie, that's amazing! Love all the details and how you got the horse's grace and strength. Do you like painting animals?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Glad you like it. Yeah, I love to. It's peaceful and special. Horses have such grace! Do you like to paint too?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Painting's great for expressing myself. I love creating art! Here's a recent self-portrait I made last week. [image: a photo of a painting of a woman with a blue face]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a woman with a blue face

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Caroline, that's great! The blue's really powerful, huh? How'd you feel while painting it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! I felt liberated and empowered doing it. Painting helps me explore my identity and be true to myself. It's definitely therapeutic.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that's great! Art's awesome for showing us who we really are and getting in touch with ourselves. What else helps you out?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie. Art gives me a sense of freedom, but so does having supportive people around, promoting LGBTQ rights and being true to myself. I want to live authentically and help others to do the same.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! That's amazing. You really care about being real and helping others. Wishing you the best on your adoption journey!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! I really appreciate it. Excited for the future! Bye!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Bye Caroline. I'm here for you. Take care of yourself.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey, Mel! How's it going? There's something I want to tell you. I went hiking last week and got into a bad spot with some people. It really bugged me, so I tried to apologize to them. [image: a photo of a woman sitting on a sign on top of a mountain]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a woman sitting on a sign on top of a mountain

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! Sorry that happened to you. It's tough when those things happen, but it's great you apologized. Takes a lot of courage and maturity! What do you think of this? [image: a photo of a plate with a bunch of flowers on it]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a plate with a bunch of flowers on it

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! That plate is awesome! Did you make it?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, I made it in pottery class yesterday. I love it! Pottery's so relaxing and creative. Have you tried it yet?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Nah, I haven't. I've been busy painting - here's something I just finished. [image: a photo of a painting of a sunset on a small easel]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a sunset on a small easel

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow Caroline, that looks amazing! Those colors are so vivid, it really looks like a real sunset. What gave you the idea to paint it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! I painted it after I visited the beach last week. Just seeing the sun dip below the horizon, all the amazing colors - it was amazing and calming. So I just had to try to capture that feeling in my painting.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a sunset over the ocean

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, the beach really inspired you. The art really took me to that moment and I can feel the serenity. You captured the sunset perfectly, so peaceful!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks Mel, really appreciate your kind words. It means a lot to me that you can feel the sense of peace and serenity. Makes me feel connected.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I feel the same way! Art is so cool like that - it connects us and helps us understand each other. I was actually just remembering yesterday, spending the day with my fam volunteering at a homeless shelter. It was hard to see how neglected some people are, but it was great to feel like we could make a difference.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel, you're amazing! Volunteering and making a difference- it's so heartwarming. You're an inspiration to us all! [image: a photo of a crowd of people walking down a street with a rainbow flag]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a crowd of people walking down a street with a rainbow flag

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! I really appreciate your help and motivation. What made you decide to transition and join the transgender community?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a bulletin board with a rainbow flag and a don't ever be afraid to

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Finding a community where I'm accepted, loved and supported has really meant a lot to me. It's made a huge difference to have people who get what I'm going through. Stuff like this mural are really special to me! [image: a photo of a building with a large eagle painted on it]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a building with a large eagle painted on it

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Caroline, glad you found a supportive community! Can you tell me more about why it's special to you?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: The rainbow flag mural is important to me as it reflects the courage and strength of the trans community. The eagle symbolizes freedom and pride, representing my own resilience and that of others.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a stained glass window with a picture of a person on a horse

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I'm in awe of your courage as a trans person. Have you made any more art lately?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a stained glass window with a person holding a key

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! I made this stained glass window to remind myself and others that within us all is the key to discovering our true potential and living our best life. [image: a photo of three stained glass windows in a church with a clock]
turns.image_caption: a photo of three stained glass windows in a church with a clock

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that looks amazing! What inspired it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks! It was made for a local church and shows time changing our lives. I made it to show my own journey as a transgender woman and how we should accept growth and change.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a large stained glass window in a church

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline!  All those colors are incredible and the story it tells is so inspiring.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a door with a stained glass window and a coat rack

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! Glad you like it. It's a symbol of togetherness, to celebrate differences and be that much closer. I'd love to make something like this next! [image: a photo of a painted sidewalk with a rainbow design on it]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painted sidewalk with a rainbow design on it

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, that's gorgeous! Where did you find it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I was out walking in my neighborhood when I came across this cool rainbow sidewalk for Pride Month. It was so vibrant and welcoming, I had to take a picture! It reminds us that love and acceptance are everywhere—even where we least expect it.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's so nice, Caroline! Art can be in the most unlikely places. Love and acceptance really can be found everywhere.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a person drawing a flower on the ground

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Agreed, Mel! Art can be a real mood-booster - I saw someone drawing on the ground the other day and it made me so happy. Creativity sure can lighten someone's day. [image: a photo of a painting of flowers and a watercolor palette]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of flowers and a watercolor palette

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that's so nice! The colors are so bright and the flowers are so pretty. Art is such a source of joy.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Mel! Art gives me so much joy. It helps me show my feelings and freeze gorgeous moments, like a bouquet of flowers. [image: a photo of a drawing of a bunch of flowers on a table]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a drawing of a bunch of flowers on a table

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, did you make that? It looks so real!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yeah, definitely! Drawing flowers is one of my faves. Appreciating nature and sharing it is great. What about you, Mel? What type of art do you love?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a drawing of a flower bouquet with a person holding it

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Painting landscapes and still life is my favorite! Nature's amazing, here's a painting I did recently. [image: a photo of a painting of a sunflower on a canvas]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a sunflower on a canvas

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, Mel! Any more paintings coming up?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I'm feeling inspired by autumn so I'm planning a few. You got any cool art projects coming up?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I'm putting together an LGBTQ art show next month and I'm gonna show my paintings. Super stoked!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that's awesome! Can't wait to see your show - the LGBTQ community needs more platforms like this!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yeah Mel, stoked! Gonna be a great night featuring LGBTQ artists and their awesome talents. We want it to spread understanding and acceptance - let's make it happen!
turns.image_caption: a photo of a poster for a concert with a man in a cowboy hat

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Melanie, great to hear from you. What's been up since we talked?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline! Since we last spoke, I took my kids to a park yesterday. They had fun exploring and playing. It was nice seeing them have a good time outdoors. Time flies, huh? What's new with you? [image: a photo of a playground with a climbing net and a slide]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a playground with a climbing net and a slide

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, your kids had so much fun at the park! Being outdoors can be really enjoyable. A lot happened since our last chat. I've been chasing my ambitions and had the chance to volunteer at an LGBTQ+ youth center. It was so gratifying to talk to similar young people. It made me remember how essential it is to be kind and show support.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That sounds great, Caroline. Volunteering is a great way to meet people. Creating community and supporting each other, especially for kids, is really important. How did you feel about your time there? Anything that sticks out to you?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I loved it. It was awesome to see how strong the young people were, with all the challenges they face. I felt fulfilled guiding and supporting them. I even got to let them know they're not alone by sharing my story. Such a powerful, emotional experience.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a table with a black table cloth and a group of people

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Was connecting with those young folks meaningful for you?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: It was so special to me. It reminded me of my own struggles in the past and how I felt alone. I was glad I could share my story and offer them support - it felt like I could make a difference.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's great. Sharing your story and support might make a difference for a long time. What do you hope to do next time?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I'm definitely carrying on volunteering at the youth center. It's an important part of my life and I've made strong connections with people there. I really believe in community and supporting each other. So I wanna keep making a difference.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's great news, Caroline! Love seeing your dedication to helping others. Any specific projects or activities you're looking forward to there?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: We're putting together a talent show for the kids next month. I'm looking forward to seeing how much fun everyone has and how proud they'll feel of their talents!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's so cool, Caroline! That's a great way to show off and be proud of everyone's skills. You know I love live music. Can't wait to hear about it! [image: a photo of a band playing on a stage in a park]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a band playing on a stage in a park

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow! Did you see that band?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, that pic was from a show I went to. It was so much fun and reminded me of how music brings us together.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a crowd of people at a concert with their hands in the air

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, what a fun moment! What's the band?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: "Summer Sounds"- The playing an awesome pop song that got everyone dancing and singing. It was so fun and lively!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That sounds great! Music brings us together and brings joy. Playing and singing let me express myself and connect with others - love it! So cathartic and uplifting.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a man playing a guitar in a recording studio

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Cool! What type of music do you play?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Guitar's mostly my thing. Playing it helps me get my emotions out.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a guitar on display in a store

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's awesome! What type of guitar? Been playing long?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I started playing acoustic guitar about five years ago; it's been a great way to express myself and escape into my emotions.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Music's amazing, isn't it? Any songs that have deep meaning for you?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yeah totally! "Brave" by Sara Bareilles has a lot of significance for me. It's about being courageous and fighting for what's right. Whenever I hear this jam, I think about the paths I've taken and the progress I've made.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's a gorgeous song, Caroline. It really fits with your journey and your determination to make a difference. Music can be so inspiring and uplifting.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a piece of paper with a drawing of a man playing a piano

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! Appreciate it. You play any instruments?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, I play clarinet! Started when I was young and it's been great. Expression of myself and a way to relax.
turns.image_caption: a photo of a sheet music with notes and a pencil

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Cool! Got any fav tunes?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I'm a fan of both classical like Bach and Mozart, as well as modern music like Ed Sheeran's "Perfect".
turns.image_caption: a photo of a laptop computer with a graph on it

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Mel, long time no chat! I had a wicked day out with the gang last weekend - we went biking and saw some pretty cool stuff. It was so refreshing, and the pic I'm sending is just stunning, eh? [image: a photo of a beach with a fence and a sunset]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a beach with a fence and a sunset

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline! It's so good to hear from you! That pic is so beautiful, the colors really pop. Biking sounds like a great way to get out in nature. We went camping with the kids a few weeks ago, had a blast exploring the forest and hiking. Nature can be so refreshing for your soul. Any plans coming up? [image: a photo of a dirt road surrounded by trees with yellow leaves]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a dirt road surrounded by trees with yellow leaves

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Melanie, that photo's amazing! I love all the yellow leaves, it looks so cozy. That sounds like fun! Seeing how excited they get for the little things is awesome, it's so contagious.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! It's awesome seeing the kids get excited learning something new about nature. Those moments make being a parent worth it. We roasted marshmallows and shared stories around the campfire. Those simple moments make the best memories. What inspires you with your volunteering?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I'm inspired seeing my work make a difference for the LGBTQ+ community. Knowing I'm helping create a more loving world is amazing. I'm really thankful for my friends, family and mentors' support. It inspires me to keep making art, too. [image: a photo of a painting of a heart on a table]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a heart on a table

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline, that looks awesome! I love how it shows the togetherness and power you were talking about. How long have you been creating art?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Since I was 17 or so. I find it soempowering and cathartic. It's amazing how art can show things that are hard to put into words. How long have you been into art?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Seven years now, and I've finally found my real muses: painting and pottery. It's so calming and satisfying. Check out my pottery creation in the pic! [image: a photo of a group of bowls and a starfish on a white surface]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a group of bowls and a starfish on a white surface

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Melanie, those bowls are amazing! They each have such cool designs. I love that you chose pottery for your art. Painting and drawing have helped me express my feelings and explore my gender identity. Creating art was really important to me during my transition - it helped me understand and accept myself. I'm so grateful.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! It has really helped me out. I love how it's both a creative outlet and a form of therapy. Have you ever thought about trying it or another art form?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: I haven't done pottery yet, but I'm game for trying new art. I might try it sometime! Check out this piece I made! [image: a photo of a painting on a easel with a red and blue background]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting on a easel with a red and blue background

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caroline! This painting is awesome. Love the red and blue. What gave you the idea?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! I made this painting to show my path as a trans woman. The red and blue are for the binary gender system, and the mix of colors means smashing that rigid thinking. It's a reminder to love my authentic self - it's taken a while to get here but I'm finally proud of who I am.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, Caro, that painting is amazing! You've made so much progress. I'm super proud of you for being your true self. What effect has the journey had on your relationships?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie. It's definitely changed them. Some close friends kept supporting me, but a few weren't able to handle it. It wasn't easy, but I'm much happier being around those who accept and love me. Now my relationships feel more genuine.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Caroline, it's got to be tough dealing with those changes. Glad you've found people who uplift and accept you! Here's to a good time at the café last weekend - they even had thoughtful signs like this! It brings me so much happiness. [image: a photo of a sign posted on a door stating that someone is not being able to leave]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a sign posted on a door stating that someone is not being able to leave

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Whoa, Mel, that sign looks serious. Did anything happen?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: The sign was just a precaution, I had a great time. But thank you for your concern, you're so thoughtful!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Phew! Glad it all worked out and you had a good time at the park!

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, it was so much fun! Those joyful moments definitely show us life's beauty.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Hey Mel, what's up? Long time no see! I just contacted my mentor for adoption advice. I'm ready to be a mom and share my love and family. It's a great feeling. Anything new with you? Anything exciting going on?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Hey Caroline! Great to hear from you! Wow, what an amazing journey. Congrats!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! I'm stoked to start this new chapter. It's been a dream to adopt and provide a safe, loving home for kids who need it. Do you have any experience with adoption, or know anyone who's gone through the process?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, a buddy of mine adopted last year. It was a long process, but now they're super happy with their new kid. Makes me feel like maybe I should do it too!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: That's great news about your friend! It can be tough, but so worth it. It's a great way to add to your family and show your love. If you ever do it, let me know — I'd love to help in any way I can.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! Appreciate your help. Got any tips for getting started on it?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yep! Do your research and find an adoption agency or lawyer. They'll help with the process and provide all the info. Gather documents like references, financial info and medical checks. Don't forget to prepare emotionally, since the wait can be hard. It's all worth it in the end though.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks for the tip, Caroline. Doing research and readying myself emotionally makes sense. I'll do that. BTW, recently I had a setback. Last month I got hurt and had to take a break from pottery, which I use for self-expression and peace.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Oh man, sorry to hear that, Melanie. I hope you're okay. Pottery's a great way to relax, so it must have been tough taking a break. Need any help?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline. It was tough, but I'm doing ok. Been reading that book you recommended a while ago and painting to keep busy.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Cool that you have creative outlets. Got any paintings to show? I'd love to check them out.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Yeah, Here's one I did last week. It's inspired by the sunsets. The colors make me feel calm. What have you been up to lately, artistically? [image: a photo of a painting of a sunset with a pink sky]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting of a sunset with a pink sky

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow Mel, that's stunning! Love the colors and the chilled-out sunset vibe. What made you paint it? I've been trying out abstract stuff recently. It's kinda freeing, just putting my feelings on the canvas without too much of a plan. It's like a cool form of self-expression.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Thanks, Caroline! I painted it because it was calming. I've done an abstract painting too, take a look! I love how art lets us get our emotions out. [image: a photo of a painting on a wall with a blue background]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a painting on a wall with a blue background

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Wow, that looks great! The blue adds so much to it. What feelings were you hoping to portray?

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I wanted a peaceful blue streaks to show tranquility. Blue calms me, so I wanted the painting to have a serene vibe while still having lots of vibrant colors.

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Yeah, it's very calming. It's awesome how art can show emotions. By the way, I went to a poetry reading last Fri - it was really powerful! Ever been to one?
turns.image_caption: a photo of a poster on a wall in a classroom

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Nope, never been to something like that. What was it about? What made it so special?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: It was a transgender poetry reading where transgender people shared their stories through poetry. It was extra special 'cause it was a safe place for self-expression and it was really empowering to hear others share and celebrate their identities. [image: a photography of a sign that says trans lives matter]
turns.image_caption: a photography of a sign that says trans lives matter

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: Wow, sounds amazing! What was the event like? Those posters are great!

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: The room was electric with energy and support! The posters were amazing, so much pride and strength! It inspired me to make some art. [image: a photo of a drawing of a woman in a dress]
turns.image_caption: a photo of a drawing of a woman in a dress

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: That's awesome, Caroline! You drew it? What does it mean to you?

turns.speaker: Caroline
turns.text: Thanks, Melanie! Yeah, I drew it. It stands for freedom and being real. It's like a nudge to always stay true to myself and embrace my womanhood.

turns.speaker: Melanie
turns.text: I love it. Showing off our true selves is the best thing ever.

turns.speaker: Caroline
